schedule.description: Baltimore freefall — Cubs in hot form

stories.title: Series Preview — Cubs at Baltimore, Game 1 of 3
stories.angle: Cubs (50-40) open a 3-game road series at Camden Yards tonight. Matthew Boyd vs. Shane Baz. First pitch 5:35 PM CT. Baltimore (42-49) has lost 8 of their last 10. Cubs are -160 favorites and 7-3 over their last 10. Cubs are 15-7 all-time vs. Baltimore with a 3-game winning streak. Second half of the season begins here.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Cubs arrive in Baltimore for a 3-game set at Camden Yards — first pitch 5:35 PM CT tonight.

Matthew Boyd vs. Shane Baz. Cubs (50-40), No. 2 NL wild card. Orioles (42-49), losers of 8 of their last 10.

The second half starts here.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Series Preview — Cubs at Baltimore, Game 1 of 3
**Facts:**
- Cubs record: 50-40 (No. 2 NL wild card)
- Orioles record: 42-49
- Venue: Oriole Park at Camden Yards, Baltimore
- First pitch: 5:35 PM CT (6:35 PM ET)
- Broadcast: MASN, MARQ; streaming Fubo
- Cubs probable: Matthew Boyd
- Orioles probable: Shane Baz
- Baltimore's last 10: 2-8 (lost 8 of last 10)
- Cubs' last 10: 7-3
- Cubs moneyline: -160 favorites; Orioles +132; O/U 9
- Cubs all-time vs. Orioles: 15-7 (68.2%), current 3-game win streak vs. BAL

stories.title: Baltimore's 8-of-10 Freefall — The Cubs Arrive at the Right Moment
stories.angle: Baltimore has lost 8 of their last 10 games and is in a 42-49 hole. The Cubs arrive at 7-3 over their last 10 and No. 2 NL wild card. Bold take on the timing — this series fell at a perfect moment for Chicago to pad its wild card cushion.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Baltimore has lost 8 of their last 10 games. The Orioles are in freefall.

The Cubs arrive at Camden Yards rolling — 7-3 over their last 10, 50-40, No. 2 NL wild card.

Timing. Is. Everything.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Baltimore's 8-of-10 Freefall
**Facts:**
- Baltimore 42-49, lost 8 of last 10 games
- Cubs 7-3 over last 10 games
- Cubs -160 betting favorites
- Orioles bullpen FIP: 3.77 (23.9% K rate) — better than Cubs bullpen
- Cubs bullpen FIP: 5.22 (20.0% K rate) — shakiest in this game

stories.title: Wild Card Snapshot — Cubs Tied with Phillies, Cardinals 1.5 Back
stories.angle: Informative standings check-in. Cubs and Phillies both at 50-40, sharing No. 2 NL wild card. Cardinals at 47-40, just 1.5 games behind. Three wild card spots available. Baltimore series is a real opportunity to separate.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Wild card check-in, July 7:

Cubs 50-40, tied for No. 2 NL wild card
Cardinals 47-40 — 1.5 games back in the race

One hot week in Baltimore turns a tight race into breathing room.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
Wild Card Snapshot
**Facts:**
- NL wild card standings (as of July 6 end of day):
- Cubs 50-40 (tied No. 2 NL wild card with Phillies)
- Phillies 50-40 (tied No. 2)
- Cardinals 47-40 (No. 4 — 1.5 GB of Cubs in wild card)
- Three NL wild card spots available
- Brewers lead NL Central: 55-33 (6 GB over Cubs for division)
- Cardinals are 1.5 games behind Cubs in wild card race (50-40 vs 47-40: win diff +3, loss diff 0 → GB = 3/2 = 1.5)
research.sources:

**Confidence:** HIGH (standings from multiple sources; GB calculation independently verified)

stories.title: Taillon Rehab Update — Return Before All-Star Break
stories.angle: Taillon threw at High-A South Bend on Sunday: 3.1 IP, 1 ER, 45 pitches. Expected to return before the All-Star break — potentially as early as the Cincinnati series (~July 11-13). The rotation is beginning to heal.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Jameson Taillon made his first rehab start Sunday at High-A South Bend — 3.1 IP, 1 ER, 45 pitches.

If all goes to plan, he's back in the Cubs rotation before the All-Star break.

The cavalry is almost here.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Taillon Rehab Update
**Facts:**
- Taillon (hamstring) made first rehab start Sunday, July 6 at High-A South Bend
- Line: 3.1 IP, 1 ER, 4 H, 2 K, 45 pitches
- Craig Counsell: potential return before All-Star break
- Possible target: Cincinnati series (~July 11-13) before ASB (July 14 start)
- Edward Cabrera (hamstring): threw a session July 6; won't be activated until after ASB
research.sources:
- Sources:
- Bleacher Nation (July 6): https://www.bleachernation.com/cubs/2026/07/06/cubs-injury-updates-taillons-start-cabreras-session-shaws-wrist/
- Cubbie Crib: https://cubbiescrib.com/cubs-injury-update-jameson-taillon-rehab-start-matt-shaw-timeline-shelby-miller
- ChiCitySports: https://www.chicitysports.com/chicago-cubs-jameson-taillon-rehab-edward-cabrera-update/
- Roundtable.io (Yahoo Sports): https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/cubs-starter-jameson-taillon-could-211507667.html
**Confidence:** HIGH (Taillon's rehab start confirmed by multiple outlets; return timeline is projection, labeled as "if all goes to plan")

stories.title: Matt Shaw Wrist — Out Until After All-Star Break
stories.angle: Craig Counsell confirmed Monday that Shaw won't return before the All-Star break despite being IL-eligible this week. Left wrist sprain not considered serious. Kevin Alcantara filling the gap.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Matt Shaw won't be back before the All-Star break.

The left wrist sprain isn't considered serious, but the Cubs are being cautious. Kevin Alcantara picks up the slack.

Post-break return is the target. Not a long-term concern.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Matt Shaw Wrist Update
**Facts:**
- Matt Shaw: left wrist sprain, on IL
- Craig Counsell confirmed (July 6) Shaw won't return before All-Star break
- Shaw would have been IL-eligible this week but team being cautious
- Kevin Alcantara + Pedro Ramirez filling in
- Injury from Cardinals series

stories.title: Pre-Game Hype — Boyd vs. Baz at Camden Yards
stories.angle: Set the stage for tonight's game. Boyd vs. Baz. Baltimore 8-of-10 losses. Cubs 7-3 over their last 10. Second half opening frame.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Matthew Boyd vs. Shane Baz. Camden Yards. 5:35 PM CT.

Baltimore has lost 8 of its last 10. The Cubs are 7-3 over theirs.

The second half of the season starts tonight. Don't miss it.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Pre-Game Hype — Boyd vs. Baz at Camden Yards
**Facts (see Stories 1 & 2 for sourcing):**
- Boyd starts for Cubs; Baz starts for Orioles
- Shane Baz: 4-8, 4.10 ERA, 89 IP, 1.36 WHIP; last-3-start FIP 2.83 (pitching his best baseball)
- Matthew Boyd: 2-1, 6.00 ERA, 31 K, 6 BB, 24 IP — recently returned from IL (left knee meniscectomy May 7)
- NOTE: Boyd's ERA (6.00) is HIGH CONFIDENCE source-wise (CBS Sports, ESPN) but may include a rocky start before his injury; not including specific ERA/record in tweet to avoid overconfidence
- Orioles bullpen advantage noted (FIP 3.77 vs. Cubs 5.22)
